channel.title: InsurTech Intelligence
channel.description: Noticias globales de insurtech, actualizadas cada 6 horas.

item.title: Chubb muestra su apuesta por Daños Materiales, Líneas Financieras y Construcción con seis nuevos contrastados fichajes
item.description: Como era previsible, José Ramón Morales sigue configurando la estructura de Chubb para lograr los ambiciosos objetivos que se ha marcado en su gestión como Regional Executive Officer Southern Region y Head of the CEMENA Underwriting Centre de la compañía.

item.title: AI Bumps Power Cost 60% as Mega U.S. Grid Fails to Hit Supply Goal
item.description: Power-hungry data centers have increased supply costs for the largest U.S. New power supplies “simply can’t keep up with the pace of data center load growth, and everyone is paying the price.” The result ramps up the pressure on a grid that’s home to Virginia’s Data Center Alley and has borne the brunt of criticism for the struggle to manage the AI boom and sufficiently protect customers from soaring costs. electric grid by more than 60%, the system watchdog said.

item.title: Executives on the Move at Arch, Allstate, Everest
item.description: I’m confident in Arch’s continued success under her leadership.” The Allstate Corporation named Christian Lown as executive vice president and chief financial officer, effective August 3. Arch Insurance North America announced the appointment of Nora Deveau as chief claims officer, effective August 1. Deveau joined Arch in 2005 and has since held roles of increasing responsibility within the claims department.

item.title: MSIG, Aon, Allianz Commercial, Willis, QBE: 20 APAC insurance people moves of the week
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Allianz Jio Re wrote US$27.6m GWP in its first full quarter
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Customers of Anthony Jones (UK) Limited urged to check their insurance policy
item.description: From 9 July 2026, the insurance broker Anthony Jones (UK) Limited (AJL) agreed to stop carrying out any regulated activity. Anthony Jones (UK) Limited acts as an insurance intermediary and is not itself an insurer or underwriter. If you purchased an insurance policy through AJL, please contact the underwriter or insurer directly to check whether your policy is valid and your cover remains in place.

item.title: Where Argentina And Spain Are Scoring Startup Goals
item.description: To do this, we put together a snapshot of recent startup funding tallies and trends for both Spain and Argentina. With the final game between Argentina and Spain approaching on Sunday, however, it’s seeming like a real perk. Argentina’s startup ecosystem has also delivered some big hits over the years.

item.title: Rise of alternative capital in casualty could position legacy market as a pillar of stability: Legacy Roundtable
item.description: A takeaway from the 90-minute session was the shifting role of alternative capital within the casualty space, with one participant noting that this influx of capital could position the legacy industry as a crucial anchor for underwriting discipline and stability over the next five years. The roundtable participants also examined the position of the legacy market within their company’s strategic objectives. In May, our sister publication Reinsurance News hosted its second Legacy Market Roundtable in Brighton, in partnership with global reinsurer Swiss Re.

item.title: Dedicated Huygens Re SPI set up for PGGM / PFZW PartnerRe quota share investment
item.description: has been established in Bermuda, which we understand will become the dedicated structure for the insurance-linked securities investment into a quota share with reinsurance firm PartnerRe for PGGM, the Dutch pension fund investor. PGGM first allocated to the Huygens structure, which we presume to be a private quota share reinsurance sidecar arrangement, back in 1999 , setting up this investment relationship with PartnerRe for the first time. By establishing a dedicated structure to house the Huygens investment strategy and its allocation to reinsurer PartnerRe, PGGM gains greater control and oversight of the investment operations and fundamentals of this reinsurance investment, we imagine.

item.title: Aon expands Vlad Bobko’s leadership role across political risk, war risk and crisis management
item.description: Bobko will take on the role of Global Head of Strategy for Political Risk, War Risk and Crisis Management within Risk Capital, while continuing his position as CEO of Central and Eastern Europe and Nordics, effective immediately. Since 2013, Aon added that Bobko has also led Crisis Management activities at Aon Global Broking Center in London, including oversight of structured credit and political risk teams. Aon plc, a global professional services firm providing risk, retirement and health solutions, has announced an expansion of Vlad Bobko’s leadership responsibilities.

item.title: Travelers continues positive performance in Q2’26 as underwriting and investment portfolio shine
item.description: Travelers attributed this to lower catastrophe losses, higher net favourable prior year reserve development, higher net investment income and a higher underlying underwriting gain. Net investment income also contributed positively to Travelers’ Q2 2026 results, rising by $128 million to reach $1.070 billion. has reported a substantial increase in net income for Q2 2026, reaching $2.208 billion, up by $699 million from the $1.509 billion recorded in the same period last year, driven by higher core income and higher net realised investment gains.

item.title: SeyRe Global announces promotion of Dinesh Salgadu to COO
item.description: Seychelles Reinsurance Global Ltd (SeyRe Global), a reinsurance company focused on providing risk solutions and services to clients and partners, has announced the promotion of Dinesh Salgadu to Chief Operating Officer (COO). According to SeyRe Global, the appointment recognises Salgadu’s extensive industry experience, strategic leadership capabilities and commitment to operational excellence. The company said he has been part of SeyRe Global since its inception and has contributed to the organisation’s growth through innovation, technical expertise and a focus on delivering reinsurance solutions.

item.title: Wife of Ryanair Passenger Partially Sucked Out of Plane Window Recalls Incident
item.description: In what Greek officials have described as a rare incident, Karovic, sitting in a window seat, was pulled partway out of the plane as his wife and others held on to him on the flight between the Greek city of Thessaloniki and Germany on July 10. Read more: Ryanair Passenger Partly Sucked From Jet After Window Breaks Greek media and airport sources said that most likely a piece of engine broke off and smashed a window early in the flight, causing the cabin to decompress. Within seconds, she saw her 61-year-old husband being sucked through a dislodged cabin window.

item.title: Man Charged With Arson for Fire at Philadelphia Islamic Center
item.description: Attorney David Metcalf announced that Vincent Lang was arrested and criminally charged with maliciously damaging or destroying by means of fire the Northeast Philadelphia Islamic Center on Tyson Avenue. A Philadelphia man has been arrested and charged with arson related a fire at a mosque in the city last week. The criminal complaint alleges that, in the early hours of July 5, 2026, Lang approached the Islamic Center building, ignited an improvised incendiary device, and threw the device into the entryway of the mosque, causing a fire inside the structure.

item.title: London Broker Howden Plots Giant Capital Raise on IPO Path
item.description: Howden Group, the London-based insurance broker that’s chasing an initial public offering by 2030, is in talks to raise several billion pounds in new private capital as it seeks to maintain its breakneck expansion. Howden has assembled a team of key lieutenants as he plots the group’s path to the top tier of insurance broking, including David Shalders, a former operations chief of the London Stock Exchange Group Plc and Susan Panuccio, who until recently oversaw finances at Rupert Murdoch’s News Corp. The slated capital raise, likely to be one of the largest ever undertaken by a private UK business, is a key pillar of founder and Chief Executive Officer David Howden’s long-term plan.

item.title: American Growth Insurance launches AI-enabled brokerage platform with $70 million funding
item.description: The post American Growth Insurance launches AI-enabled brokerage platform with $70 million funding appeared first on Insurtech Insights . American Growth Insurance (AGI) has launched an AI-enabled insurance brokerage growth platform backed by nearly $70 million in committed equity funding from Rockbridge Growth Equity and venture capital studio Atomic . “American Growth Insurance is building out the next-generation, national insurance brokerage platform.

item.title: Rippling launches direct benefits integrations with Guardian, MetLife and Unum
item.description: Ray Russell, AVP of Platform Integration at Unum, said: “That structure dramatically improves data accuracy, reduces rework, and gives us confidence that eligibility and enrollment changes are flowing correctly every time.” New Rippling customers implementing benefits with Guardian, MetLife or Unum will be eligible to use the direct connections, while existing customers will transition through a phased migration process over the next  12 months . Rippling has launched a new benefits administration programme that connects its platform directly with insurance carriers Guardian , MetLife and Unum in an effort to reduce manual data handling and improve accuracy in employee benefits management. Bradd Chignoli, Head of Regional Business at MetLife, said: “Collaborations like Rippling’s Preferred Carrier partnership help reduce friction and create more connected experiences for employees, making it easier for employers and brokers to support their people while staying focused on running and growing their business.” Unum said the direct API connectivity model would improve data accuracy and reduce operational rework.

item.title: PremFina secures £400 million debt facility from Lloyds
item.description: UK insurance premium finance provider PremFina has secured a £400 million senior debt facility from Lloyds to support the continued growth of its loan book. The facility complements the £100 million junior capital facility PremFina secured from Waterfall Asset Management in March 2026, bringing the two facilities to a combined £500 million. PremFina said its loan book has grown by more than 300% over the past 18 months.

item.title: Opera Tech Ventures backs Alpaca again
item.description: Opera Tech Ventures , the venture capital arm of BNP Paribas, has participated in a new $135 million funding round for brokerage infrastructure provider Alpaca. Peak XV led the round, with participation from Elefund, Opera Tech Ventures, Unbound and other investors. Opera Tech Ventures also participated in that round.

item.title: Aviva and Age UK launch mid-retirement support pilot
item.description: Aviva and Age UK said its findings could support the development of a government-backed national program for people in mid-retirement. Aviva and Age UK are launching a pilot program to help retirees manage their finances and make their pension savings last longer. It is essential that support is provided, and this pilot will explore the best way of delivering it.

item.title: Canopius Names Brown Head of US Claims
item.description: Brown will be joining August 3, and she will report to incoming Canopius Group Chief Claims Officer Steve Parry. She most recently served as head of complex and specialty claims at Munich Re Specialty North America. Prior to that, she held senior claims leadership roles at Markel.

item.title: Scientists Affirm Climate Change Fuels Extreme Weather
item.description: Extreme weather comes in many types, and some of them are easier than others to analyze for climate influence. This “extreme event impact attribution” work is significantly more complicated than conducting weather-and-climate analysis alone. Human-induced changes to the global climate have “a direct and well-understood impact” on extreme heat and rainfall, according to a landmark new U.S.

item.title: What Home Age Actually Tells Us About Claims — And What It Doesn’t
item.description: With more homeowners living in older houses, it’s worth taking a closer look at what home age actually tells us about claims. Here’s what our claims data actually shows. Agents who understand this, and can explain it plainly, are the ones having a more honest conversation about what insurance is actually for.

item.title: Deep Dive: Understanding Data Center Perils
item.description: An eastward shift in tornado alley is coinciding with the buildout of data centers in places like Northern Virginia, Texas, Illinois and Georgia, risk experts revealed during a recent reinsurance industry seminar. with the middle of the country colored red and labeled “Traditional Tornado Alley.” A neighboring dark yellow section of similar width and length just to the east labeled “New Tornado Alley” was dotted with large blue bubbles to indicate locations with 100 or more data centers planned or constructed. A particularly eye-catching slide presented during a recent Casualty Actuarial Society meeting showed a map of the U.S.

item.title: QBE promotes Angus Cheung to market lead for hull and liability in North Asia
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Aon appoints David Crofts to lead global capital solutions for financial sponsors
item.description: Global professional services firm Aon has announced the appointment of David Crofts as Global Head of Capital Solutions for Financial Sponsors, effective 1 August 2026. In his new role, Crofts will work with financial sponsors, sovereign wealth funds, hedge funds and other capital providers seeking access to insurance-related opportunities, including investments in property and casualty insurance and reinsurance businesses, life insurance and wealth-related opportunities. Crofts rejoined Aon in 2025 as Head of Financial Sponsors for EMEA, having previously spent nearly six years with the firm as a Managing Director in its Global Risk Consulting business.

item.title: Emily Yoo joins SiriusPoint as Chief Operating Officer
item.description: SiriusPoint, a specialty insurer and reinsurer based in Bermuda, has appointed Emily Yoo to the newly created role of Chief Operating Officer (COO), effective August 10th, 2026, based in New York. Scott Egan, Chief Executive Officer, SiriusPoint, commented on the appointment, “Emily is a highly respected leader with deep industry experience and a strong track record. Yoo will join SiriusPoint’s executive leadership team, and be responsible for technology, claims, transformation, and operational excellence.

item.title: AGI launches as AI-enabled insurance brokerage growth platform
item.description: American Growth Insurance (AGI) has launched an AI-enabled insurance brokerage growth platform, supported by nearly $70 million in committed equity funding from Rockbridge Growth Equity, a partnership-oriented middle-market private equity firm, and venture capital studio Atomic. Brian Morgan, CEO of AGI, commented, “American Growth Insurance is building out the next-generation, national insurance brokerage platform. “Our platform is designed for partner brokerage firms to build long-term growth founded on intelligent use of AI, cultural alignment, collaboration, and enhanced client service.” Morgan holds more than 30 years of experience building public, private, and private equity-backed insurance brokerages.

item.title: White House Unveils AI Clearinghouse for Cybersecurity Risks
item.description: The Trump administration is moving to strengthen coordination on artificial intelligence cybersecurity through a new industry clearinghouse to improve the detection and patching of network vulnerabilities, especially in open-source software. Still, it remains unclear if the White House’s more recent heavy-handed approach is a permanent feature of its AI policy and what that means for any new releases on the horizon. The clearinghouse, named Gold Eagle, was rolled out earlier this month by the Treasury Department, Department of Homeland Security and the Pentagon, in consultation with AI companies, according to National Cyber Director Sean Cairncross, who briefed reporters on the initiative July 14.

item.title: US Aims to Set Guardrails for Autonomous Vehicle Behavior
item.description: The Trump administration is working to develop new safety requirements governing how autonomous vehicles behave on the road as the industry contends with a series of safety mishaps. NHTSA initially plans to seek comment from the public and industry stakeholders with the goal of identifying “behavioral competencies” for autonomous vehicles, with tests to measure performance. The goal is to craft objective safety standards “so that a manufacturer who is producing the vehicle can know with certainty whether or not the vehicle meets the requirements,” Morrison said.

item.title: Viewpoint: The AI Ransomware That Couldn’t Get Paid
item.description: A ransomware attack that ran from start to finish without a single human directing it, an AI agent alone at every stage. Notice instead that the attack could never have paid out, even for a victim who did everything right. Ordinary ransomware works like a burglar who changes your lock and keeps a spare key, because selling that spare key back to you is the entire business model.

item.title: Mutual of Enumclaw Names Scott Roddy CFO
item.description: Source: Mutual of Enumclaw.) Mutual of Enumclaw (Enumclaw, Wash.) has appointed Scott Roddy Chief Financial Officer of Mutual of Enumclaw Insurance Holding Company. Roddy joins the company as Mutual of Enumclaw advances its long-term growth strategy through the corporate structure established by its holding company, according to a company statement. “Scott’s appointment underscores the momentum behind our growth strategy and the opportunities ahead for Mutual of Enumclaw,” comments Bob Otis, President and CEO, Mutual of Enumclaw.

item.title: Germania Mutual Deploys Guidewire AI Assistant
item.description: (Image credit: IIR/Gemini.) Germania Mutual Insurance (Ayton, Ontario) has deployed Guidewire (San Mateo, Calif.) ProNavigator to support its underwriting and claims teams. The mutual insurer is using ProNavigator, Guidewire’s AI assistant, to help employees access company knowledge, documents and operational context within daily workflows, according to a joint statement from the companies. Germania Mutual has run its operations on Guidewire since 2021.

item.title: APAC (re)insurers poised to seize AI chip opportunity – yet data gaps, cat risks, and a capacity squeeze loom
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: The Ghost in State Farm's Machine
item.description: State Farm's sweeping cuts to agent compensation signal how private equity thinking now shapes even mutual insurers' operating models. State Farm just told 19,000 captive agents the deal has changed . State Farm is a policyholder-owned mutual—the largest in the country—not a private equity play.

item.title: Texas Floods, Canada Smoke and Western Heat Batter US
item.description: More than 75 people have been rescued in Texas as floodwaters rise, while Canadian wildfire smoke is choking skies across the Midwest and Northeast, including New York, and heat continues to fuel wildfire risk in California. “Our primary focus right now and throughout the remainder of this torrential rain is saving lives.” The flooding comes as multiple extreme weather hazards stretch across North America, from wildfire smoke blanketing major cities in Canada and the eastern US to heat and elevated fire danger across the West. Torrential rain has flooded parts of western and central Texas, where as much as 9 inches (23 centimeters) fell in just a few hours.

item.title: US Takes Aim at Autonomous Car Mishaps in Safety Rulemaking Push
item.description: The Trump administration is working to develop new safety requirements governing how autonomous vehicles behave on the road in a bid to address mishaps that have marred the early track record of self-driving cars. “We want to set a bit of a mark that ‘no, this is not acceptable,’ and this is where we are going to be having discussions.” Related: Companies Must Address Self-Driving Car Interference With Emergency Vehicles, NHTSA Says The push to address mishaps is part of a broader effort by the agency to help self-driving cars reach the road in larger numbers, in part by removing safety requirements that were written with human drivers in mind. Officials aim to finalize the new requirements before the end of President Donald Trump’s current term, Jonathan Morrison, the National Highway Traffic Safety Administration’s top official, said in an interview.

item.title: Starbucks Defeats Shareholder Suit Over US, China Sales Drops
item.description: A federal judge dismissed a lawsuit accusing Starbucks of defrauding shareholders by intentionally concealing declining sales in the United States and China, its largest markets. (Reporting by Stempel in New York; Editing by Chizu Nomiyama) Get the latest insurance news sent straight to your inbox.

item.title: Two Trailers Containing $1.3M in Stolen Copper Wire Recovered in Illinois
item.description: The Cook County Sheriff’s Police Organized Retail Crime Unit recently reported on X that two stolen trailers containing more than $1.3 million in copper wire were recovered. Last month, investigators received information that a trailer containing about $300,000 in copper wire, which had been reported stolen in Pine Hill, Alabama, was being tracked in the 2500 block of East Higgins Road in unincorporated Elk Grove Township, Illinois. When investigators arrived at the truck yard, they found the trailer containing the copper wire.

item.title: Fast Replies Boost Hiring Chances, Study Shows
item.description: Analyzing 11.6 million marketplace interactions and a series of experiments involving both job candidates and service providers, the researchers found no evidence that delaying a response improves hiring prospects. “People have this intuition that playing hard to get is somehow useful,” said On Amir, a professor at the UC San Diego Rady School of Management and co-author of the study. “We find the opposite is true.” Speed matters because it signals responsiveness The study combined real-world marketplace data from Fiverr, a platform connecting employers with freelancers, with three main experiments involving more than 3,600 participants and five supplemental studies involving another 5,000 participants.

item.title: Wellness, Exercise Programs Key to Keeping Workers Healthy
item.description: Targeted exercise and comprehensive wellness programs can aid workers in their careers as they age, a recent study out of Finland concluded. The researchers found that work ability can be strengthened by improving health-related behaviors and working conditions by promoting sleep, mental health, exercise and a healthy lifestyle, along with reducing unnecessary work demands and empowering workers. The other was a follow-up study of more than 2,300 social welfare and health care workers.

item.title: FSB Regional Consultative Group for Sub-Saharan Africa meets in Mauritius
item.description: The Financial Stability Board (FSB) Regional Consultative Group for Sub-Saharan Africa (RCG SSA) met on 16-17 July 2026 in Mauritius, hosted by the Bank of Mauritius. Co-chaired by Lesetja Kganyago, Governor of the South African Reserve Bank, and Denny Kalyalya, Governor of the Bank of Zambia, the meeting covered a range of topics, including: Members also discussed the FSB’s 2026 work priorities and regional contributions. The meeting brought together senior officials from central banks, financial authorities, and regulatory bodies in the region to discuss key financial stability topics.

item.title: ILS is key for mobilising risk capital for energy transition investment: Beazley
item.description: A new study by Beazley, the London headquartered specialty insurance and reinsurance company, has highlighted how insurance-linked securities (ILS) and various alternative risk transfer solutions are key for mobilising risk capital for the energy transition. Alternative risk transfer solutions are key to mobilising that capital and managing the risks associated with it,” Beazley explained. Beazley’s latest Spotlight on Energy Transformation 2026 observes that the insurance industry is becoming a core enabler of the energy transition, particularly given that it does more than absorbs losses, and helps turn uncertainty into viable, investable projects.

item.title: Allstate’s pre-tax catastrophe losses for Q2’26 reach $1.72bn
item.description: Allstate begun the new annual aggregate risk period, that its Sanders Re program catastrophe bonds and excess of loss reinsurance are subject to, with $870 million of pre-tax catastrophe losses in April 2026 . Then following a relatively low month of catastrophe losses in May, at $289 million pre-tax, Allstate’s total pre-tax cat losses then rose to $1.16 billion, Now, combined with June’s $563 million, Allstate’s total catastrophe losses for Q2 2026 are $1.72 billion, or $1.36 billion, after-tax. This is a slight decline from last year , where Allstate reported estimated catastrophe losses of $1.99 billion, or $1.57 billion after tax, for the second quarter of 2025.

item.title: Reinsurance operations must evolve to remove constraining inefficiencies: INTX CEO Lewis
item.description: Rob Lewis, CEO of INTX Insurance Software, said reinsurance operations remain constrained by fragmented architecture and manual workflows, arguing that platforms must unify data, workflows and intelligence across the economics of risk to address significant inefficiencies. In an interview with Reinsurance News, Lewis highlighted that while reinsurance has evolved into a primary lever for capital efficiency, growth and profitability for many organisations, the systems supporting global reinsurance operations have failed to keep pace. The result is a fragmented ecosystem with underwriting in one system, claims in another, billing elsewhere, and reinsurance managed across spreadsheets and disconnected tools.” Lewis noted that modern reinsurance platforms must be able to reflect constant change, including reinsurers entering and exiting programmes, adjustments to loss layers, evolving commission structures, and real-time visibility into exposures and recoverables.

item.title: Howden acquires Irish financial advisory business Thomond Asset Management
item.description: Global insurance and reinsurance broking group Howden expands its footprint in Ireland with the acquisition of Thomond Asset Management, the trading name of Folk Asset Management Limited, a financial advisory business headquartered in Limerick. Dermot Gaskin, Managing Director of Financial Advisory, Howden, said: “Thomond Asset Management has built a strong wealth management business in Limerick City, with values that closely align to our own. I’m delighted to welcome Tom, Neal, David and the wider team to Howden.” Co-founders of Thomond Asset Management commented: “We’re excited to join Howden as its first Financial Advisory acquisition in Limerick City.

item.title: AIG promotes Anouk Sireude to Head of Marine, Pacific
item.description: (AIG), a global insurer and reinsurer, has promoted Anouk Sireude to Head of Marine, Pacific. Dimitry Zilberud, Head of Marine – APAC at AIG, commented, “Anouk’s leadership, commercial insight and deep understanding of the marine market have been instrumental in the growth of our portfolio across the Pacific. In her expanded role, Sireude will be responsible for Inland and Ocean Marine insurance across Australia and New Zealand.

item.title: In Michigan, 100K Live Downstream of Old, Decrepit Dams
item.description: Like more than 100,000 other Michiganders living downstream of potentially hazardous dams that are often aging, undersized or neglected, Korbecki is left to wonder how long LaBarge will hold. “Why should you be allowed to own a dam if you can’t maintain it?” Six years after dam failures near Midland prompted unsuccessful efforts to boost oversight of Michigan’s dams, safety shortcomings were laid bare in dramatic fashion again this spring as floods triggered another spate of failures and near-misses that cost taxpayers at least $5 million and likely far more. Like LaBarge, dozens of other dams deteriorate out of the public spotlight, thanks in part to federal policies that let owners shield safety records and state laws that deem Michigan dams safe when other states would consider them dangerous.

item.title: Northeast Texas Counties Work to Form Groundwater District
item.description: More than a dozen East Texas counties are working to create a groundwater conservation district that would prevent over-pumping water from the aquifer below their communities. On Tuesday, Gregg County Commissioners voted to approve a resolution adding the county to a growing number of East Texas communities interested in developing the Northeast Texas Groundwater Conservation District. Leaders in northeast Texas want to create a groundwater conservation district to protect East Texans from losing all their groundwater.

item.title: Texas Contractor Fined $343K Following Excavation Collapse
item.description: Department of Labor investigation concluded that a Houston utility construction company exposed employees to safety hazards after an excavation collapse hospitalized a worker. The department’s Occupational Safety and Health Administration initiated an investigation into Blazey Construction Services LLC following the incident, which occurred while crews were installing sewer and water pipes for a residential development in Alvin, Texas. OSHA determined that the employer failed to adequately protect the excavation, provide a safe means of egress, and report the hospitalization within 24 hours as required by federal law.
